description: Sibling of evidence-submission-decision-v1 for the OTHER irreversible terminal action: conceding the dispute via Stripe's `POST /v1/disputes/{id}/close` (accept liability, funds stay withdrawn). Closing is final and cannot be reopened, so the decision to concede is gated the same way — a human principal authorizes it before the recipe calls /close. Kept as a distinct type (not a reject-branch of the submission decision) so the audit reads cleanly: 'authorized to CONCEDE' and 'authorized to CONTEST' are different acts with different liability and should not share a record. | The performer (analyst) records why concession is the right call; the principal (lead) renders the verdict. SEPARATION OF DUTIES IS ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see evidence-submission-decision-v1): the engine only blocks a performer-who-isn't-the-principal from rendering the verdict; it does NOT reject principal == performer self-commitment. The orchestrator must provision distinct analyst and lead identities and must not self-commit. | Same F-787 deployment note as the submission decision: defaultGateMode:principal, but the type cannot forbid a gateMode:auto override — the orchestrator must route concessions through the human gate at the recipe layer.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...; older docs show dp_) being conceded.

disputeRef.description: recordId of the chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 this concession descends from.

amount.description: Amount being written off (stays withdrawn) on concession, major units.

concessionBasis.description: Why concession is chosen over contesting. fraud_confirmed = the merchant agrees the charge was fraudulent.

evidenceDueBy.description: Carried from the filing for deadline visibility.

rationale.description: Analyst's substantiation for conceding rather than contesting.

acknowledgedIrreversible.description: Explicit acknowledgement that /close is terminal and the funds stay withdrawn. Must be true.

writeOffAmount.description: Optional booked write-off amount if it differs from the disputed amount (e.g. partial recovery elsewhere).
